source_pr.title: test: merge executor's serial tests to other tests
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?

Problem Summary: After https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/pull/30692, all the tests are run in serial. The names of `*_serial_test.go` are no longer appropriate.

### What is changed and how it works?

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [ ] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- bugfix or new feature needs a release note -->

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: - t.Skip("race test for this case takes too long time") | tk.MustQuery("select upper(group_concat(distinct value order by 1)) from test.t;").Check(testkit.Rows("ABC,DEF")) (executor/analyze_test.go)
failure_signature: - t.Skip("race test for this case takes too long time") | tk.MustQuery("select upper(group_concat(distinct value order by 1)) from test.t;").Check(testkit.Rows("ABC,DEF")) (executor/analyze_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE: This PR mainly reorganizes/renames executor serial test files after the test suite was forced to run serially (e.g., merging `*_serial_test.go` into regular test files). It does not contain a clear targeted stabilization change for a specific flaky failure signature, so we keep the classification conservative.
fix_pattern: N/A (insufficient evidence; no targeted stabilization change identified).

evidence: evidence: - t.Skip("race test for this case takes too long time") | tk.MustQuery("select upper(group_concat(distinct value order by 1)) from test.t;").Check(testkit.Rows("ABC,DEF")) (executor/analyze_test.go)
